investigation_summary: Channel 4 2023: garment piece-rate ~3¢, 18-h shifts disputed by company but filmed. Thousands of new SKUs daily with no verified environmental accounting. Designer copyright judgments (e.g., $150k trio 2023). UFLPA / House China Select Committee concerns on de minimis customs gap. CBC lead over limits in tested items.

profile.parent: Roadget Business Pte. Ltd. (Singapore); founder Xu Yangtian
profile.tax_summary: Private — minimal public financials. Singapore hub; China export rebates. IPO attempts delayed — U.S. disclosure friction reported.
profile.legal_summary: Mass independent-designer infringement pattern — settlements and judgments. U.S. House China committee flags UFLPA documentation risk. UK IPO governance scrutiny 2024. Privacy class actions (TikTok-parallels alleged).
profile.labor_summary: Channel 4 undercover wages and hours. No published supplier list or third-party audit suite. Uyghur forced-labor documentation gaps under U.S. law cited by policy makers.
profile.environmental_summary: Ultra-fast model (thousands of styles/day). No CDP. Synthetic fabrics / microfibers. Chemical excess lead in CBC tests.
profile.political_summary: Lobbying to preserve $800 de minimis exemption (with Temu peers) per press accounts.
profile.product_health: Lead and other hazardous substances over limits in CBC Marketplace tests (children's accessories, etc.).
profile.executive_summary: Founder low-profile; company disputes labor TV findings — methodology archived by broadcaster.
